description: Complete genome registry for the Pretext world text engine. Every significant entity in the repository cataloged as a genome with full codon sequences, family taxonomy, and inter-genome connections.

codon_legend.TXT: Text content — prose, poetry, corpus data, narrative payload
codon_legend.IMG: Visual element — canvas, SVG, image, screenshot
codon_legend.CTRL: Control flow — event handling, routing, lifecycle management
codon_legend.EVT: Event/interaction — user input, touch, keyboard, mouse
codon_legend.DAT: Data structure — JSON, arrays, maps, typed arrays
codon_legend.DOC: Documentation — README, comments, annotations, companion papers
codon_legend.CSS: Styling — visual design system, layout, animation, theming
codon_legend.DOM: DOM manipulation — element creation, classList, innerHTML
codon_legend.NET: Network — fetch, WebSocket, import, module loading

ORCA.name: ORCA Echolocation Family
ORCA.description: Raycaster-based text exploration engines. Share a common DNA of CRT overlay aesthetics, amber/cyan palette, LiDAR scanning mechanics, Web Audio echolocation, WASD navigation, and poem-collection gameplay. The Appalachian physics narrative thread runs through the elder members.

CAVE.name: The Cave — Immersive Environments
CAVE.description: Pretext-powered 3D text environments exploring consciousness, perception, and spatial typography. All consume the core layout engine via prepare()/layout() and render text in immersive canvas-based worlds. The Cave is the phenomenological laboratory.

LAYOUT.name: Layout Demonstrations
LAYOUT.description: Pure demonstrations of the prepare() → layout() pipeline for practical UI patterns. No DOM measurement in the render loop. Proves the engine works for real product scenarios: chat bubbles, accordions, masonry grids, rich text, editorial spreads.

TYPOGRAPHIC.name: Typographic Systems
TYPOGRAPHIC.description: Explorations of typography as a system: justification algorithms, particle-driven ASCII art, and full editorial page composition. These push beyond layout into the aesthetics and mathematics of typesetting.

ENGINE.name: Core Engine
ENGINE.description: The DNA backbone. These are the source-of-truth TypeScript modules that implement the text layout algorithm: segmentation, measurement, line breaking, bidi metadata. Everything else in the repo ultimately depends on this family.

INSTRUMENT.name: Pedagogical Instruments
INSTRUMENT.description: Standalone interactive tools for exploring cognitive, perceptual, and textual phenomena. Not strictly demos of the layout engine — these are experimental interfaces that treat text as a medium for investigating attention, reading, and consciousness.

GENOMA.name: The Genome System
GENOMA.description: The meta-system itself. The GENOMA editor, scope, docs, and this registry. Software that describes software. The ouroboros.

RESEARCH.name: Research & Documentation
RESEARCH.description: Companion papers, development notes, research documents, and the CULTURE-MIND-BRAIN scholarly corpus. The intellectual substrate that gives the code its theoretical gravity.

genomes.name: Layout Engine
genomes.description: The heart. Exports prepare(), prepareWithSegments(), layout(), layoutWithLines(), layoutNextLine(), walkLineRanges(), setLocale(), clearCache(). The resize hot path: no DOM reads, no canvas calls, no string work. Everything else in this repo exists to feed or consume this module.

sequence.payload: prepare() — opaque fast-path handle from text + font → prepared object
layout() — resize hot path: prepared + width + lineHeight → { height, lineCount }
layoutWithLines() — rich path: returns line boundaries with text, width, start/end cursors
layoutNextLine() — streaming escape hatch for variable-width userland layout
walkLineRanges() — batch geometry API: no string materialization, browser-like widths
prepareWithSegments() — rich path with segment arrays and bidi metadata
setLocale() — retargets word segmenter, clears caches
clearCache() — resets segment metrics cache
Segment metrics cache: Map<font, Map<segment, metrics>>
imports from ./analysis.js, ./measurement.js, ./line-break.js, ./bidi.js

genomes.name: Text Analysis
genomes.description: The preprocessing brain. Normalization, Intl.Segmenter-based word segmentation, glue rules (NBSP, NNBSP, WJ), soft hyphen handling, tab/hard-break detection, CJK grapheme splitting with kinsoku merging, Arabic no-space punctuation clusters, URL-like run detection, contextual escaped quote clusters, numeric/time-range runs.

sequence.payload: Text normalization and segmentation pipeline
8 break kinds: normal, collapsible-space, preserved-space, tab, NBSP-glue, ZWSP, soft-hyphen, hard-break
CJK grapheme splitting + kinsoku prohibited punctuation merging
Arabic no-space punctuation clusters and mark handling
URL-like run detection: path through query introducer, then query string
Contextual escaped quote clusters: \"word\"
Numeric/time-range runs: 7:00-9:00, २४×७
Word and grapheme segmenters hoisted at module scope
Segment model with break-kind enum

genomes.name: Canvas Measurement
genomes.description: The sensory organ. Canvas-based text measurement runtime. Manages the shared segment metrics cache (Map<font, Map<segment, metrics>>). Handles emoji correction (auto-detected per font size, constant per emoji grapheme, effectively font-independent). Engine-profile shims for browser-specific tolerance: 0.005 for Chromium/Gecko, 1/64 for Safari/WebKit.

sequence.payload: Canvas measureText() wrapper with caching
Emoji correction: auto-detected per font size
Engine-profile shims: Chromium 0.005, Safari 1/64 tolerance
Segment metrics cache: Map<font, Map<segment, {width, graphemeWidths}>>
Grapheme widths lazily populated
system-ui unsafe: canvas and DOM resolve different fonts on macOS

genomes.name: Line Break Walker
genomes.description: The rhythm section. Internal line-walking core shared by the rich layout APIs and the hot-path line counter. Handles word-boundary wrapping, grapheme-boundary fallback for narrow widths, soft hyphen breaking with visible trailing hyphen, and the whitespace mode split (normal vs pre-wrap).

sequence.payload: Line-walking core: advances through segments fitting to width
Word-boundary wrapping with grapheme-boundary fallback
Soft hyphen: invisible when unbroken, visible trailing '-' when broken
Pre-wrap mode: preserved spaces, tabs (default tab stops), hard breaks
CSS target: white-space:normal, word-break:normal, overflow-wrap:break-word

genomes.name: Bidi Metadata
genomes.description: The directional compass. Simplified bidi metadata helper for the rich prepareWithSegments() path. Bidi levels stay as custom-rendering metadata only — the opaque fast prepare() handle does not pay for bidi metadata that layout() does not consume. Line breaking itself does not read levels.

sequence.payload: Bidi level assignment for segments
Levels on rich path only, not on fast path
Custom-rendering metadata, not consumed by layout()

genomes.name: BAT-MIND // Claudar
genomes.description: The eldest ORCA instrument. A 26×26 maze raycaster where walls are textured with Pretext-wrapped lines of Richard Wilbur's 'Mind'. Uses prepareWithSegments() + layoutNextLine() to generate wall textures. Full spatial audio engine with convolution reverb simulating cave acoustics, echolocation delay line modulated by closest wall distance, and distance-aware low-pass beacon filter. Poem fragments are scattered as collectible spatial objects.

sequence.payload: ORCA // BAT-MIND — A CAVE OF COGNITION
Raycaster engine: 26×26 map, DDA ray-wall intersection, textured columns
prepareWithSegments() + layoutNextLine() → wall texture lines from Wilbur corpus
import { prepareWithSegments, layoutNextLine } from './dist/layout.js'
Convolution reverb (5s cave impulse), echolocation delay line (0.035-0.58s modulated by wall distance)
Distance-aware low-pass beacon filter: far=350Hz, close=3500Hz with harmonic partials
Rhythmic proximity pulse: square wave tick accelerates near target
Finale: harmonic field (A3 major 9th), delay opens to 1.8s, space dissolves
WASD + pointer lock (desktop), dual-zone touch (mobile), SPACE to scan
CRT scanline overlay (3px pitch, 0.18 opacity), crosshair, pulse ring VFX
Minimap: 120×120 canvas, discovered cells revealed progressively
Richard Wilbur — 'Mind': 13 lines scattered as spatial poem nodes
Amber (#ff8c00) / Cyan (#00ffcc) / Pink (#e024c3) palette, JetBrains Mono + Cormorant Garamond
Boot screen with ORCA branding, HUD (surface/bearing/echo-return/progress)

genomes.name: ORCA // Aidar
genomes.description: The autonomous ORCA variant. Same LiDAR geometry scanner as orcadar but with an AUTO-PILOT mode that navigates the world automatically, seeking poem fragments without human input. Features progress dots UI showing collection state. External TypeScript module for the engine.

sequence.payload: ORCA // GEOMETRY SCANNER with AUTO-PILOT
2D top-down LiDAR scanner with 360° ray fan
Autopilot pathfinding: autonomous navigation toward nearest uncollected fragment
WASD movement + mouse/touch scan, autopilot toggle
Web Audio echolocation pings with stereo panning
12 poem fragments scattered in procedural maze
CRT overlay, amber/cyan palette, Cormorant Garamond poem display
Progress dots UI: 12 dots showing collection progress
aidar.ts — external TypeScript engine module

genomes.name: ENGRAM // Archive Decrypt
genomes.description: The cinematic ORCA variant. Branded as 'ENGRAM // ARCHIVE' with a dramatic boot sequence featuring typewriter text and a flash-white transition. Same LiDAR core but with cryptographic/archival theming. Desktop/mobile-aware hint system.

sequence.payload: ENGRAM // ARCHIVE DECRYPT
2D LiDAR with cinematic flash overlay transition (cyan→fade)
Typewriter boot sequence with staged text reveal
Responsive: desktop WASD + click, mobile split-touch with thumb regions
12 poem fragments (same Wilbur corpus), archival/decrypt framing
Bat emoji favicon, mobile-first poem overlay with gradient fade
jumpar.ts — external TypeScript engine module

genomes.name: ORCA Shield 18 // Holler Optics
genomes.description: The patriarch. A 2470-line standalone Appalachian physics engine. Dual-layer architecture: a top-down topo grid (32×32) with 5 terrain zones (Shack, Creek, Ridge, Puddle, Cave) AND a first-person raycaster LIDAR mode. Features a forensic Crime Scene Processing Report ontology with 10 evidence items. Uses Tone.js for audio. The deepest lore in the repo.

sequence.payload: ORCA // BLUE_HOUR_LIDAR_v18 — HOLLER OPTICS // SYSTEM_18
Dual-layer: 32×32 topo grid (canvas) + first-person raycaster (canvas)
3 flashlight modes: WIDE, PRISM, LASER with different spread patterns
MOUNTAIN_LORE: 5 zone narratives (shack, creek, ridge, puddle, cave)
EVIDENCE_ONTOLOGY: 10 items (PRISM, GLASSES, LAMBCHOP, ANTLER, GLOW-BALL, OIL, HUBCAP, SHADOW, COLLAR, DOG HOUSE)
CSPR Forensic Report generator with ASCII art borders
collectEvidence() / getCSPRReport() evidence management system
Tone.js: triangle synth + sine drone, zone-specific pitch mapping
Dual-zone mobile joysticks (MOVE + LOOK), D-pad arrows, teleport landmarks
CRT scanline overlay, minimap (100×100), LIDAR minimap (150×150)
Holler palette: Moonshine Gold, Rust, Steel Blue, Peru, Indigo, Dark Slate Gray
Appalachian physics narrative: Grandpa's quantum optics, Edgar the missing hound
ripple-ai.js — external AI helper script

genomes.name: WORD-ENGINE // Orcadar
genomes.description: The open-world ORCA. A massive 400×400 grid (4800×4800px world) 2D LiDAR scanner. No raycaster — instead a persistent 'LiDAR map' canvas accumulates discovered geometry over time. Full Web Audio engine with convolution reverb, echolocation delay line, and spatial beacon system. Floor text canvas renders Wilbur's poem in Cormorant Garamond revealed only by LiDAR sweeps.

sequence.payload: ORCA // GEOMETRY SCANNER — open-world LiDAR
400×400 grid (4800×4800 world), persistent map canvas accumulator
Floor text canvas: poem lines rendered in Cormorant Garamond at 24px, revealed by LiDAR
Procedural maze: 600 random walls (10-40 length, 1-3 thickness), poem clearings
Full Web Audio: convolution reverb (4s), delay line (0.05-0.6s), stereo panning
Algorithmic beat: distance-ratio-driven tick rate, only fires when target illuminated
Beacon pings: E major pentatonic (329-554 Hz), octave shifts by line index
Collision detection, procedural world generation, poem node placement with min-distance
WASD + mouse (desktop), dual-touch joystick (mobile)
Richard Wilbur — 'Mind' (12 lines), completion state with algorithmic pulse animation
Completion: poem centers, Cormorant Garamond 26px, hue-rotate pulse, cyan/green alternating

genomes.name: WORD-ENGINE // Walldar
genomes.description: The Culture//Mind//Brain raycaster. Branded as 'Enter the Extended Mind' — explicitly themed around the Cartesian Breach and disciplinary matrices. Uses Pretext's layout engine via external walldar.ts module. JetBrains Mono + Cormorant Garamond dual font system. LiDAR scanner HUD with minimap.

sequence.payload: CULTURE // MIND // BRAIN — Enter the Extended Mind
Raycaster with Pretext-wrapped wall textures
walldar.ts: Pretext layout integration for wall text generation
Disciplinary matrices, somatic markers, Cartesian boundary theming
WASD + mouse aim + click pulse, mobile drag/swipe/tap zones
Crosshair (+), minimap (120×120), CRT overlay
JetBrains Mono UI + Cormorant Garamond poem, amber/cyan/pink palette
walldar.ts module (type='module')

genomes.name: Cognitive Lanes
genomes.description: A 3-lane parallel reading interface. Text flows vertically in three synchronized columns with focal-point depth-of-field blur. Features a Toll Booth friction mechanism that forces structural pauses at marked rows, simulating cognitive load thresholds. Speech synthesis reads the center lane. Flow speed slider maps to congestion status levels (LOW/MODERATE/HIGH).

sequence.payload: 3-Lane Parallel Reading Interface
Focal-point rendering: distance-from-center → opacity + blur (depth of field)
Toll Booth friction: structural halt for 1.2s at marked rows (attention tax)
Speech synthesis: center lane auto-spoken at focal crossing, rate scales with flow speed
Flow speed slider (0.2–3.0), pause/play toggle, speech toggle
12-row corpus: [left, center, right] word triples, toll booth flags
Parchment palette (#f4f3ed), Lora serif + JetBrains Mono, TailwindCSS
Dynamic row spawning/cleanup, flash overlay for toll booth events
Congestion status levels: LOW (fluid) → MODERATE (accelerated) → HIGH (saccade overload)

genomes.name: Dynamic Layout
genomes.description: The flagship layout demo. A fixed-height editorial spread with obstacle-aware title routing, continuous two-column flow, and live logo-driven reflow. The real consumer of the rich line API (layoutNextLine).

sequence.payload: Dynamic Layout — editorial spread with variable-width line routing
layoutNextLine() for variable-width obstacle-aware line stepping
Continuous two-column flow with title routing around obstacles
Live logo-driven reflow: dragging the logo recalculates column widths
imports from ../../src/layout.ts

genomes.name: Bubbles
genomes.description: Width-tight multiline message bubbles using walkLineRanges(). Less wasted area than standard text-width boxes. The shrinkwrap demo.

sequence.payload: Bubbles — width-tight multiline chat bubbles
walkLineRanges() for shrinkwrap width calculation without string materialization
Optimal bubble width: max(lineWidths) instead of container width
imports from ../../src/layout.ts

genomes.name: GENOMA Editor
genomes.description: The genome manipulation interface. Create, edit, mutate, recombine, and express genomes. Codon management with type/payload/state. JSON import/export. Phenotype expression engine renders genomes as live HTML. Mutation (random codon swap) and recombination (splice two genomes) operations.

sequence.payload: GENOMA v2 — Genome Editor
Codon CRUD: add, edit, delete, reorder codon sequences
Phenotype expression: translateGenome() → rendered HTML from codons
Mutation: random codon state swap (active/dormant/silent)
Recombination: splice two genomes at random crossover point
JSON import/export: loadGenomeFromJSON(), exportGenome()
Phenotype capture: html2canvas screenshot of expressed genome
Codon types: TXT, HDR, LNK, IMG, FN, VAR, EVT, CSS, GFX, SND, CTRL, DOM, DAT, DOC
Dark theme with rose accent (#f43f5e), glassmorphism panels

genomes.name: GENOMA Scope
genomes.description: The genome browser. High-level visualization of multiple genomes simultaneously. Translates each genome's spliced sequence into a rendered HTML phenotype preview. Search, filter, inspect.

sequence.payload: GENOMA Scope — multi-genome visual browser
_translate(): spliced codon sequence → rendered HTML phenotype
loadData(): initializes genome collection with event bindings
Card grid with phenotype previews, click-to-inspect
Dark theme, genome cards with codon count badges
